transcript.clean_text: stage three of the toreno adriatico are not expected to throw up too many surprises well how wrong will we fabulous course ahead of us run to tourney with the terrain just starting to undulate a little bit more starting off in a small village of merlot where the team buses had to park 13 kilometers away and the small tracks welcoming the riders before the start once underway just one categorized climb and an intermediate sprint before our finale that itself with a rising series of cobbles and twisty turns involving five roundabouts at the very last so signing on for duty in beautiful sunshine clear skies have been a characteristic of the race so far and the nine celsius temperatures of the day prior on stage two to soviet chile a little bit warmer around 14 celsius it was hot to get in the break and the man who wanted to be there of course was davide baies he wearing the green jersey after taking the only climb to the race so far doubled up 100 in the competition and will be in green again tomorrow with brother mattie baies was also in the break two from dronehopper with zardini up there rostelli from bardiani also complementing with vanderhorn of inter martial wanted face joined by maestro well the break was out there and we were just enjoying some of the scenery along the way this place with the deepest well in italy dead center of that cathedral dead center of everyone's thoughts uae team emirates what on earth was going on with just under 50 kilometers to go the breakaway was now less than a minute away and teleport catcher had a plan this was apparently to work for pascal ackerman to tire out some of the sprinters out there leaving aquaman a chance to take glory well when there's some intermediate bonus seconds up for grabs steli pikachu is always hungry for more every second counts he says but he pushed off with mark solaire and julian alaphilippe marking them out for quick step alpha vinyl and indeed bridging over with teo gagan hart decided to make a move he and the company of mikkel lander who sadly faded leaving four up front pushing on while we were climbing up towards a plateau that did for lander the tailgate heart showing this sort of form that brought him glory in the zeroed italian he made it four up front and then we were wondering what on earth would happen our philippe probably looking out for himself on the day we weren't too sure talipacher had a battle plan clearly the pace was not to his liking but indeed neither was the gap ultimately it was never going to be extended and so keeping his powder dry for yet another battling day as the terrain will roll into walls tomorrow we had a kind of a wall today and the obstacles were coming thick and fast in terms of the roundabouts they all had to be navigated and the chase that had been engaged by the sprinters teams had fatigued a few and it meant that the battle plans had to be rearranged at the very last anna de mar was determined that he was going to make amends for what's been a disappointing start to the season and in fact his launch was a spectacular one he was in a very very good place as gamieri guided him where he needed to be that was a question of damage limitation for just about everybody else mark cavanish not in a position for this one still suffering from a cold but it was absolutely white hot as we hit the cobbles on the rise to the finale demars clearly was the wheel to follow and kelley buen got onto that and indeed came through for a fabulous win all of koi again from jumbo visma fast finishing once more he just can't help but pick up podiums but the top step is what you want and that belonged to caleb ewan supreme on that final turn which came extremely late he bowed his time it very very well indeed buwani also in the frame for a good result ackermann was there having had all that work done for him by tally pakacha but just faded to finish outside the top five caleb's power there for all to see and he's not afraid to big himself up on occasion a little guy standing very very tall on that top step today fantastic win for euan damar in second place said he was very disappointed coy third another podium for him bohani looking good tim miller that's a victory he's in the chickamina of course tomorrow with that good position today best spot though has to be p1 that of course was caleb what about the overall pippo garner still loud and proud on top 11 clear of ever nepal but talibacher three more bonus seconds for him today cuts his march into 14 seconds overall a lot of racing to come and with the walls that faces tomorrow can this man stay in blue by the end you better believe it he proved in uae he can climb and climbing he will be doing as we head off towards valente well a ramp of 11 to deal with don't worry too much about it it'll be 21 the day after but you have to take it three times and that is gonna hurt well their hurt is our enjoyment it's going to be a heck of a day's racing you can be certain join us for stage four it'll be special on the torino adriatica you
